Title: Zambry rejects claims Malaysia's Israel policy is anti-Semitic
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abdul Kadir said Malaysia's refusal to recognise Israel was not linked to anti-Semitism, rejecting such claims.
Speaking after a public lecture by Columbia University professor Joseph A. Massad titled 'Muslim Identity in a Globalised World' today, Zambry said he concurred with Massad's rejection of claims that criticism of Israel amounted to anti-Semitism.
He said the criticism against Malaysia was linked to Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim's stance on Israel, adding that Putrajaya could not accept Israel's actions, which Massad had described as a "genocidal state".
"But of course, for us to accept whatever they do, regarding what the professor mentioned just now about the 'genocidal state', I don't think we should accept it," Zambry told reporters.
Malaysia does not have diplomatic relations with Israel and does not recognise the country. It recognises Palestine and has long supported Palestinian statehood.
Massad, meanwhile, commended Malaysia and its leaders for refusing to bow to what he described as pressure from the United States to normalise relations with Israel.
He said Washington had long pressured Muslim-majority and Arab countries to establish diplomatic ties with Israel.
"Any criticism of Israel is legitimate and is not anti-Semitic at all.
"Not having diplomatic relations with a genocidal state is a statement against genocide; it is not against Jews," he said
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: European corporate outlook continues to improve as earnings season gathers steam
Article:
LONDON: Estimates for European blue-chip companies' earnings have improved, the latest LSEG I/B/E/S data showed on Wednesday, as soaring energy-sector profits are set to help deliver the best growth in more than three years.
Companies on Europe's benchmark STOXX 600 index are expected to report earnings growth of 17.3 per cent in the second quarter, according to the data that is based on results from 77 companies and market estimates for those that are yet to report.
Although all but one sector — healthcare — are expected to post profit growth, the aggregate rate is seen at a more moderate 7.2 per cent when excluding the energy sector's 122.6 per cent estimated growth rate.
Revenues for European blue-chips are seen growing by 11.5 per cent, their best rate since the last quarter of 2022, finally breaking a streak of negative or flat growth in the past 13 quarters.
Shares of TotalEnergies and Repsol climbed on Thursday after their second-quarter results were met positively by the market, while Franco-Italian chipmaker STMicroelectronics fell after its earnings missed market estimates.
Investors will also be closely monitoring the index next week, as the bulk of the STOXX 600 companies — including Europe's biggest banks, industrials and carmakers — are due to report their results.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Zecon awards RM328mil solar contract to subsidiary
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Zecon Bhd's subsidiary Zecon Renewables (Sarawak) Sdn Bhd has awarded a RM328 million engineering, procurement, construction and commissioning (EPCC) contract to Zecon Renewables Sdn Bhd.
The contract covers the development of a 100MWac large-scale agrivoltaic solar PV facility at Kota Petra Green Technology Park in Sarawak.
Zecon Renewables is Zecon Group's dedicated EPCC and renewable energy outfit.
Zecon Renewables will deliver the full EPCC scope for the 100MWac facility.
This comprises the solar PV plant itself, connection facilities, a proposed 130-metre bridge over the Serai River, and site infrastructure and earthworks at Lot 471 within the technology park.
The company's renewable energy track record also includes a 2MWac rooftop solar installation at Hospital Pakar Kanak-Kanak Tuanku Ampuan Besar Tuanku Aishah Rohani in Cheras.
The 100MWac Phase 1 marks the first major activity under the technology park masterplan, an important milestone that signals the shift from planning and approvals to execution.
As the anchor renewable energy project within the park, it sets the template for how future phases will be structured, financed and delivered.
A further 300MWac of solar capacity remains available under Zecon's electricity licence for solar power, issued by Sarawak's Utility and Telecommunication Ministry in February 2025 and valid for 30 years.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Selangor allocates RM40.5mil for Petaling Jaya flood mitigation
Article:
SHAH ALAM: The Selangor government has allocated RM40.5 million to execute immediate and medium-term flood mitigation projects across critical hotspots under the Petaling Jaya City Council's (MBPJ) jurisdiction.
State Infrastructure and Agriculture Committee chairman Datuk Izham Hashim said the initiative is a proactive move to address increasingly frequent flash floods caused by climate change, rapid development, and high-intensity rainfall.
"The execution of this mitigation plan is expected to reduce flood risks while boosting urban readiness and resilience against climate change challenges," he said in a statement.
He said the plan also encompasses 39 projects to fortify flood defences in high-risk areas, including Seksyen 51A, Kampung Cempaka, and Jalan Kuala Selangor (FT54).
Under the action phase, a total of 26 work scopes involving an allocation of RM20.4 million will be carried out, while the medium-term phase comprising 13 work scopes is valued at RM20.1 million.
Key measures across these phases include upgrading drainage capacity, constructing outlet structures, maintaining rivers and flood retention ponds, and installing flood-warning sirens at critical locations.
Izham said the plan is being executed through a strategic partnership involving the Drainage and Irrigation Department, MBPJ, the Public Works Department, and other relevant technical agencies to ensure effective implementation and maximum benefit for residents.
He said the state is committed to integrated flood management to ensure funds are utilised optimally to protect vulnerable communities across Selangor. (https://www.nst.com.my/news/nation/2026/07/1495145/pj-traders-demand-flood-solution-watch)
It was reported that Medan Selera 223 in Petaling Jaya had endured their fourth flash flood on Saturday.
This left traders fuming at the recurring incident and want answers from the authorities on what long-term measures are being planned to prevent another devastating flood in their area.
A visit by the New Straits Times to Jalan 223 and the nearby industrial zone on Wednesday found several drains still clogged with debris from Saturday's flash floods.
Some sections of the drainage network also appeared damaged due to ageing and lack of maintenance.
Taman Medan state constituency coordinator Halimey Abu Bakar said the proposed project to upgrade the area was discussed between the state government, DID and MBPJ.
He said that existing drainage and nearby river capacity could no longer be expanded to cope with heavy rainwater run-off, which often flows into the area from neighbouring Kuala Lumpur.
"DID and the state government both agreed that we need to fix it because the drain or the river cannot be enlarged anymore."
Halimey said following recent severe downpours that overwhelmed local infrastructure, the area needs to be upgraded, where the drains were filled with rocks and sediment.
He added that local authorities and MBPJ will engage with the affected traders regarding the issues and their plans.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Online child safety: Over 1,500 harmful posts involving children removed
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A total of 1,550 offensive online content items involving children have been removed by licensed service providers following enforcement action by the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) between Jan 1, 2022 and June 30 this year.
The Communications Ministry said the content was taken down following requests by the MCMC as part of enforcement efforts, including technical assistance, information sharing and digital forensic analysis to support investigations by the relevant authorities.
"The MCMC also conducts proactive monitoring of harmful content and takes regulatory action under the Communications and Multimedia Act 1998 (Act 588) and the Online Safety Act 2025 (Act 866)," the ministry said in a written reply to Senator Michael Mujah Lihan in the Dewan Negara today.
Michael had asked about the number of cases involving the misuse of artificial intelligence (AI) and the dissemination of child sexual abuse material (CSAM) online, as well as the government's measures to address the issue.
The ministry said statistics on criminal cases involving AI misuse and the dissemination of CSAM are maintained by the relevant enforcement agencies, particularly the police, based on their respective jurisdictions.
It said the Online Safety Act 2025 (ONSA), which came into force on Jan 1, requires licensed application service providers and licensed content application service providers to ensure their platforms are safer, including by addressing child sexual abuse material.
The ministry said the Child Protection Code, which came into force on June 1 this year, requires licensed service providers to implement protection measures, including age verification and a ban on social media account registration by users below 16 years old.
It also said the Safe Internet Campaign had been carried out in 10,303 schools and institutions of higher learning, as well as through 2,143 community programmes, reaching 2,190,104 participants nationwide as of June 30.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: BN to unveil Negri Sembilan election manifesto on Friday
Article:
REMBAU: Barisan Nasional will unveil its manifesto for the Negri Sembilan election tomorrow night, with a focus on continuing development initiatives that were left unfinished.
BN deputy president Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan said the manifesto would build on development plans that were stalled after the coalition lost power in the 2018 General Election.
"To give a little preview before tomorrow night's launch, the manifesto is about continuing the work that was interrupted after the BN government lost in 2018.
"The foundation is already there. We just want to strengthen it and continue what was left unfinished after BN's defeat in 2018," he told reporters after the Santai Bersama Tok Mat Hasan programme in Pedas today.
Also present was BN's Chembong candidate, Datuk Zaifulbahri Idris.
Responding to claims that the Negri Sembilan BN manifesto had adopted elements of BN's Sabah state election manifesto, Mohamad dismissed the allegation as baseless and described it as psychological warfare by the certain quarters.
"The claims circulating that we copied or took it from Sabah election are not true. We have never done such a thing.
"It is merely psychological warfare by them, accusing us of plagiarising or adopting the Sabah state election manifesto," said the Negri Sembilan BN chairman.
On another matter, Mohamad said BN has yet to decide on its "poster boy" or the candidate who would lead the state administration if the coalition is given the mandate to form the state government.
He said the party's immediate priority was ensuring its entire election machinery remained focused on campaigning to secure victory.
"Let us focus on campaigning properly first. After that, I am confident the party will be able to decide who should be appointed as the 'poster boy' or who will lead the state administration if BN wins this election.
"The important thing is that we should not be overly focused on the poster boy issue while neglecting the campaign.
"So, let us make sure we win. Let's get past the post first."
Meanwhile, commenting on Parti Wawasan Negara president Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin's reference to the electoral understanding between Perikatan Nasional and BN as "Perikatan-Barisan Nasional (PBN)", Mohamad said:
"He can say whatever he wants. We are not forming a new coalition.
"Barisan Nasional has no intention or desire to establish another coalition. Our coalition remains Barisan Nasional.
"We are contesting this election based on an electoral understanding. There is no collaboration of that sort. They are their party, we are our party, but there is an understanding between us to avoid splitting the Malay vote.
"At this point, as BN deputy president, I do not see or expect any new collaboration to be formed by BN. We are merely working under an electoral understanding to face this election," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Attempt to pressure Malaysia on Israel policy exposes US selective moralism, double standards
Article:
LETTER:
The recent attempt by eight members of the United States Congress to pressure Malaysia into reversing its policy on Israeli nationals is an unacceptable act of political intimidation.
In a letter to US Secretary of State Marco Rubio, the lawmakers reportedly demanded that Malaysia change its position within 15 days.
They also called for a review of American security and economic relations with Malaysia, including the possible suspension of International Military Education and Training funding.
This is not diplomacy. It is coercion, an attempt by foreign legislators to use financial and military leverage to interfere in the sovereign decisions of an independent country.
The lawmakers described Malaysia's immigration policy as "antisemitic" and accused the Malaysian government of "hunting down and removing Jews".
Such language is reckless, inflammatory and deeply misleading.
Malaysia is not conducting a campaign against Jewish people. Malaysia is enforcing a national policy concerning citizens of a state with which it has no diplomatic relations.
There is a fundamental difference between Judaism, Jewish identity, Israeli citizenship, Zionism and the policies of the Israeli government. Any serious legislator should understand these distinctions.
To deliberately erase these differences is to weaponise the accusation of antisemitism for political purposes.
Antisemitism is real. It is dangerous. It must be opposed wherever it appears. Hatred, discrimination or violence against Jewish people because of their religion or ethnicity is morally indefensible.
But criticism of Israel, opposition to Zionism, rejection of Israeli military actions or restrictions involving Israeli passport holders cannot automatically be classified as antisemitism.
To make that automatic equation is intellectually dishonest. It also weakens the struggle against genuine antisemitism because the term becomes a political shield used to silence criticism of a state.
Malaysia's policy does not declare that Jewish people are unwelcome because they are Jewish. It addresses Israeli nationals and Israeli passport holders within the framework of Malaysia's foreign relations, immigration laws and national security considerations.
Malaysia has no diplomatic relations with Israel. Israeli passport holders generally require specific approval to enter the country. This is a long-established national position, not a policy invented overnight against a religious community.
Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim has correctly stated that Malaysia will not be intimidated into abandoning this decades-old policy and that the country is not violating international law by maintaining it.
The right to regulate the entry of foreign nationals is a basic attribute of state sovereignty.
The United States itself exercises broad authority to deny visas, impose travel restrictions, deport foreign nationals and sanction individuals from countries it considers hostile to its interests.
Washington cannot claim an unlimited right to control its own borders while denying Malaysia the same sovereign authority.
No foreign national possesses an automatic right to enter Malaysia. Entry into Malaysia is subject to Malaysian law, immigration procedures and national interests.
American lawmakers may disagree with Malaysia's policy. They may express their position through proper diplomatic channels. But they have no legitimate authority to impose deadlines, threaten funding or demand obedience from Malaysia.
Malaysia is not an American dependency. Malaysia is not a protectorate of Washington. Malaysia does not require permission from members of the US Congress to determine who may enter Malaysian territory.
The demand to consider suspending military training assistance is especially revealing. It suggests that programmes presented as security cooperation can quickly be converted into instruments of political pressure.
If assistance is conditional upon Malaysia surrendering its independent foreign policy, then it is not genuine partnership. It is leverage.
[[nid:1495693]]
The amount of American military assistance received by Malaysia does not purchase Malaysia's sovereignty. It does not give Washington ownership over Malaysia's immigration laws, diplomatic recognition policies or position on Palestine.
Malaysia must therefore carefully reassess any foreign assistance arrangement that can be used to extract political compliance.
National dignity cannot be traded for training allocations, defence programmes or financial incentives.
A sovereign nation must be prepared to reject assistance when the hidden price is silence, submission or complicity.
The congressional letter also accused Malaysia of maintaining open ties with Hamas and alleged that Hamas fighters had trained on Malaysian soil. The lawmakers claimed that Malaysia was "welcoming terrorists while targeting Jews".
These are grave accusations. They should not be thrown into a political letter without credible, publicly verifiable evidence.
Malaysia has consistently maintained relations with Palestinian political and humanitarian representatives because Malaysia supports the legitimate rights of the Palestinian people.
Engagement with Palestinian actors does not give American lawmakers the right to label Malaysia a supporter of terrorism.
The United States does not possess an exclusive moral authority to determine which resistance movements, governments or political organisations the rest of the world may communicate with.
History has repeatedly shown that groups once condemned as terrorists by powerful governments have later become parties to negotiations, peace processes and recognised political arrangements.
Diplomatic engagement is not equivalent to endorsement of every action taken by every party.
The eight lawmakers speak loudly about the alleged discrimination faced by Israeli citizens. Where is their comparable outrage over Palestinian families displaced from their homes?
Where is their ultimatum concerning the killing of Palestinian civilians? Where is the threat to suspend American military support when Palestinian children are killed, hospitals are attacked or humanitarian access is restricted?
Why is the sovereign immigration policy of Malaysia treated as a greater moral emergency than the continuing destruction imposed upon the Palestinian people?
This selective morality is precisely why much of the world has lost confidence in Washington's claim to defend a rules-based international order.
Rules cannot be applied only to weaker states. Human rights cannot be invoked only when they serve American allies.
[[nid:1495692]]
International law cannot be enforced against geopolitical opponents while being diluted, obstructed or ignored when Israel is involved.
Malaysia's position on Israel did not emerge from racial or religious hatred. It arose from a long-standing opposition to occupation, dispossession and the denial of Palestinian self-determination.
The historical persecution of Jewish communities in Europe was a terrible crime against humanity. That history must be remembered with honesty and respect.
But the suffering of Jewish people cannot be appropriated to protect the policies of the Israeli state from criticism.
Nor should the memory of the Holocaust be used to intimidate countries that oppose Israeli occupation and military aggression.
When every restriction against Israeli nationals is described as an attack on Jews, the identity of an entire religious and ethnic community is wrongly fused with the conduct of a government.
That itself is dangerous. Jewish people are diverse. They hold different political views. Many Jewish scholars, activists, religious figures and human rights defenders have criticised Zionism, occupation and Israeli military actions.
Israel does not speak for every Jewish person in the world. The US Congress does not have the authority to declare that opposition to Israeli state policy constitutes hatred of Jewish people.
Malaysia should respond calmly, formally and unequivocally. The government must reject the 15-day ultimatum. It must defend Malaysia's sovereign authority over immigration and foreign policy.
It must demand that the lawmakers withdraw the inflammatory allegation that Malaysia is "hunting down Jews". It should also require evidence for claims concerning alleged terrorist training on Malaysian territory.
Malaysia's diplomatic response should emphasise that the country rejects antisemitism, Islamophobia, racism and all forms of religious hatred.
At the same time, Malaysia must make clear that accusations of antisemitism will not be allowed to silence its principled support for Palestine.
Malaysians must remain united on this question. This is larger than one immigration controversy.
It concerns whether Malaysia will be permitted to maintain an independent foreign policy or whether foreign politicians can dictate national decisions through threats and financial pressure.
Malaysia seeks constructive relations with the people and government of the United States.
We value cooperation founded upon mutual respect. But friendship does not mean obedience. Partnership does not mean subordination. Military assistance does not create the right to interfere in Malaysia's sovereign affairs.
The eight members of Congress should withdraw their threats and abandon the irresponsible attempt to equate Malaysia's policy toward Israeli nationals with hostility toward Jewish people.
They should also reflect on the damage caused when legitimate concerns about antisemitism are manipulated to defend Israel from accountability.
Malaysia will determine its own foreign policy, control its own borders, continue to defend the Palestinian people and will not surrender its sovereignty because foreign legislators issue an ultimatum.
The message from the Malaysian people must be unmistakable: Do not weaponise antisemitism. Do not bully Malaysia. Do not demand that Malaysia abandon Palestine in exchange for American assistance. Malaysia's sovereignty is not for sale.
MOHD AZMI ABDUL HAMID
President
Malaysian Consultative Council of Islamic Organisation (MAPIM)
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 'Johor aura': BN relies on southern success to convince Negri Sembilan voters
Article:
KUALA PILAH: Barisan Nasional (BN) is banking on the "Johor aura" to win over voters in the Negri Sembilan election, Wanita Umno chief Datuk Seri Dr Noraini Ahmad said.
She said BN believed its track record in Johor would help convince voters to support the coalition's candidates.
Noraini said many voters she had met during the campaign compared the current administration with the period when BN governed, with Johor often cited as an example of effective governance.
"We are trying to bring the Johor aura to Negri Sembilan and we hope it will produce positive results.
"We hope voters will give BN the opportunity to implement the plans and policies that we will propose.
"They are not empty promises but realistic initiatives that can be delivered," she told reporters during the Ziarah Kasih programme with BN's Pilah candidate, Lisa Mat Yasin.
Noraini said feedback from Wanita Umno's grassroots machinery indicated growing support for BN candidates after several days of campaigning.
She said party workers had reported a positive reception from voters across the state, adding that residents had welcomed BN candidates during house-to-house visits.
"We will continue engaging with voters throughout the campaign. Today, in Pilah, we visited several homes and the response was very encouraging. People welcomed our candidate and opened their doors to BN," she said.
Noraini said Wanita Umno's immediate priority was to mobilise its election machinery to help secure victory for BN candidates.
"Our focus is to ensure the Wanita Umno machinery works hard to help BN win and form the next Negri Sembilan government," she said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] MACC records statements from 10 witnesses in KWAP eFishery probe
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has recorded statements from 10 witnesses, comprising senior officials from the Retirement Fund (Incorporated) (KWAP) and the Finance Ministry, as part of its investigation into KWAP's RM200 million investment in Indonesian aquaculture technology company eFishery.
MACC chief commissioner Datuk Seri Abd Halim Aman said the commission had established a special investigation team led by the Investigation Division senior director, with officers from its Investigation, Legal and Prosecution, and Governance Investigation divisions.
The investigation was officially launched on July 17, while investigators entered KWAP's premises on July 20 to begin gathering evidence.
"As of July 22, statements from all 10 witnesses have been recorded.
"The witnesses comprise senior officers from KWAP and the Finance Ministry involved in the investment decision-making process.
"Investigators are still at the preliminary stage of the probe," Halim said in a statement today.
He said investigators are now analysing the witnesses' statements and reviewing documents seized during the operation.
"They will also trace financial transactions, including cash transaction reports (CTR) and suspicious transaction reports (STR). More witnesses, including members of KWAP's Finance Committee and Investment Panel, are expected to be called.
"Documents under scrutiny include the initial and final proposal papers, site visit reports, meeting minutes relating to eFishery, due diligence reports, investment procedures and KWAP's financial records," he said.
Halim said the probe covers allegations of corruption, abuse of power and misappropriation, while also examining governance issues involving financial procedures, legal compliance and the decision-making process behind KWAP's investment in eFishery.
He said that the investigation was still at an early stage and that it was premature to disclose any findings.
"Several people overseas have been identified to assist in the investigation, with the MACC set to apply for Mutual Legal Assistance in Criminal Matters through the Attorney-General's Chambers to obtain evidence from foreign witnesses.
"I have instructed the investigation team to conduct a thorough, transparent, fair and comprehensive probe from every possible angle.
"We will leave no stone unturned in our search for evidence," he said, urging all parties to
allow the MACC the time and space
needed to complete the investigation.
It was reported that investigators from the MACC visited KWAP headquarters here earlier this week as part of the investigation into the fund's reported RM200 million loss following its investment in Indonesian aquaculture technology company eFishery.
It is understood that investigators were at the office on Monday and Tuesday, where they collected documents related to the investment.
The MACC probe is understood to cover KWAP's governance, decision-making processes and compliance in approving the investment, as well as whether any criminal misconduct or corruption was involved in the transaction.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Police bust love scam syndicate preying Japanese victims, arrest 7 foreigners
Article:
ALOR STAR: Police have busted an online love scam syndicate preying on Japanese victims, following a raid on a shop lot in the Kristal Light Industrial Park here last Sunday that led to the arrest of seven Chinese nationals.
Kedah police chief Datuk Adzli Abu Shah said the raid, which was conducted at 1.40pm on July 20, was based on intelligence that the premises were being used as a call centre for the online scam syndicate.
He said the male suspects, aged between 21 and 30, are believed to have played a role in identifying potential victims before passing their details to another group.
"Investigations revealed that they were recruited in China, through overseas job offers advertised on the Douyin app.
"The men were brought into Malaysia and placed at the premises, with salaries ranging from RM2,000 to RM3,000, to scam victims in Japan, " he told reporters here today.
He said the syndicate is believed to have begun operations since mid-June, using the LINE and Langmate apps to create fake identities to deceive victims.
Information obtained from seized computers revealed that 19 Japanese nationals have fallen prey to the syndicate.
Police are continuing to determine the actual number of victims as well as identify the syndicate's upper-level network.
"We also seized 58 items believed to have been used in the criminal activities, including 34 mobile phones, six laptops, one monitor, one CPU, one router and a modem, all estimated at RM50,000," he said.
He said the suspects have been remanded from July 21 to 24 to assist investigations under Section 420 of the Penal Code, Section 120B of the Penal Code, and Section 6(1)(c) of the Immigration Act 1959/63.
Adzli said police have also identified a Malaysian premises owner believed to be linked to the syndicate, but he remains at large and efforts to track him down are still ongoing.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Tabung Haji RCI report needs cabinet nod before Parliament tabling
Article:
REMBAU: The release of the Royal Commission of Inquiry (RCI) report on Tabung Haji to the public is subject to cabinet approval before it can be tabled in Parliament.
Foreign Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan said any paper to be brought to Parliament must first be considered and approved by the cabinet.
"It is up to the Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Law and Institutional Reform) Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said to bring it to the cabinet before it can be tabled in Parliament.
"Any paper to be presented in Parliament must first go through the cabinet and obtain its approval," he told reporters after attending the Santai Bersama Tok Mat Hasan programme in Pedas today.
He was responding to questions on whether the Tabung Haji RCI report would be made public.
Mohamad said the matter was subject to the established process and did not pose any issue.
Earlier, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the government was prepared to
release
the final RCI report on Tabung Haji to allow the public to examine allegations involving the disposal of the agency's strategic assets.
He said he would consult Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar on whether the report could be released today or after next Wednesday's Cabinet meeting.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Fallen construction structure disrupts KTM Komuter, ETS services at Batang Benar
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: KTM Komuter Klang Valley and Electric Train Service (ETS) operations were disrupted today after a wooden structure from a third-party construction site fell onto the railway tracks near Batang Benar, damaging the overhead power system.
Keretapi Tanah Melayu Bhd (KTMB) said the structure struck the overhead line system between Batang Benar and Labu stations, disrupting the power supply and affecting train operations.
As a result, KTM Komuter services from Pulau Sebang towards KL Sentral are terminating at Seremban Station, while services heading to KL Sentral or Batu Caves are operating only from Bangi Station.
"There are currently no KTM Komuter services operating between Bangi and Seremban stations. Affected passengers are advised to use alternative modes of transportation to continue their journeys," it said in a statement.
To assist affected commuters, KTMB has deployed three shuttle buses operating between Seremban, Tiroi, Batang Benar, Labu and Bangi stations.
For ETS passengers, KTMB has also arranged seven shuttle buses at KL Sentral, Bandar Tasik Selatan, Kajang and Seremban stations.
"ETS passengers who choose not to continue their journey from their departure station are entitled to a full refund," read the statement.
KTMB said its preliminary investigation found that the Public Works Department contractor carrying out the wayleave project had failed to comply with the required safety procedures.
It said the contractor did not submit a complete work schedule and failed to notify KTMB of the actual timing of the bridge construction works carried out on the day of the incident.
"Under the Site Entry Permit Standard Operating Procedure (SOP), three KTMB technical representatives - from the Regional Engineer, Signalling and Communications, and Electrification departments - must be present on-site for monitoring once full approval has been granted and all required documentation has been submitted," the statement said.
KTMB added that its technical team is working to repair the damaged overhead line system to restore train services as quickly as possible.
"KTMB apologises for the inconvenience caused by this unforeseen incident. The safety of passengers and train operations remain our highest priority," it said.
Passengers are advised to check KTMB's official social media platforms or consult station staff for the latest service updates.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Singapore woman gets back valuables after leaving bag on bus
Article:
SINGAPORE: A woman who accidentally left a bag containing valuables worth $32,000 (RM101,000) on a Singapore bus was reunited with her belongings on the same day, thanks to the quick actions of transport staff and a fellow passenger.
In a Facebook post, SBS Transit said the passenger, identified only as K, realised she had left her bag on the bus only after alighting from Service 853 at a bus stop in Ang Mo Kio.
"In tears, she approached our team at the nearby Ang Mo Kio Interchange for help.
"Although SBS Transit does not operate Service 853, Senior Assistant Interchange Supervisor Nithyananthan Palne (Nathan) immediately sprang into action.
"He contacted Tower Transit Singapore to try to trace the missing bag," it said.
Within 20 minutes, the bus operator said Tower Transit confirmed that another passenger had found the bag and handed it over.
Although the bag had been found, SBS Transit said K did not have any money with her at the time, prompting Senior Interchange Supervisor Alex Tan to step in and personally book and pay for a taxi ride to Yishun Interchange so she could retrieve the bag.
"Two days later, both K and her daughter called to thank us and even extended an invitation to their wedding in Kuala Lumpur," Nathan was quoted as saying.
A week later, K returned to Ang Mo Kio Interchange to personally thank Nathan and Alex, while also reimbursing Alex for the taxi fare he had paid out of pocket.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: No one should be shielded if Tabung Haji RCI finds wrongdoing, says Asyraf
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Umno secretary-general Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki has called for no one to be shielded if the Royal Commission of Inquiry (RCI) report on Tabung Haji finds evidence of misconduct, corruption or abuse of power.
Commenting on Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim's statement that the RCI report would be made public soon, Asyraf said Malaysians wanted more than just disclosure – they wanted firm action against those found guilty.
"That is good. What is important is that action must be taken. Do not just create perceptions.
"If there was wrongdoing, any form of misconduct, corruption or irregularities, the people have been waiting for a long time.
"Do not let it remain at the level of perception. Those responsible must be brought to justice. No one should be protected," he said.
He said the release of the RCI report would only be meaningful if followed by legal action against those found responsible, regardless of their position or influence.
Anwar had earlier said the government was prepared to
release
the final RCI report on Tabung Haji to allow the public to examine allegations surrounding the disposal of the agency's strategic assets.
He said he would consult Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar on whether the report could be released today or after next Wednesday's cabinet meeting.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Man claims trial to 12 counts of armed robbery, cheating
Article:
KLANG: A man pleaded not guilty at the Sessions and magistrate's courts here today to 12 charges of armed robbery, theft and cheating involving jewellery and cash worth more than RM90,000 between 2022 and this year.
B. Murugan, 52, entered his plea after the charges were read out before Sessions Court Judge Mujib Saroji and magistrates Siti Zubaidah Mamat, A. Karthiyayini and Nor Azilah Mat.
At the Sessions Court, Murugan was charged with two counts of armed robbery involving two 74-year-old women in Taman Sri Sentosa and Bandar Sultan Sulaiman on Jan 3, 2024 and Feb 10 this year.
The charges were framed under Section 392 of the Penal Code, read with Section 397, which carries a maximum jail term of 14 years, a fine or whipping upon conviction.
At the magistrate's court, he faced five counts of cheating, four counts of theft and one count of robbery allegedly committed in Klang between 2022 and 2025.
The charges were framed under Sections 379, 380, 392 and 417 of the Penal Code.
Deputy public prosecutors Nor Suhaili Abu Bakar, Mohammad Uzzair Rosman and Salman Alfarisi Nawawi, together with prosecuting officer Inspector Mohamad Rabiul Awal Ishak, appeared for the prosecution.
The prosecution proposed bail ranging from RM4,000 to RM12,000 for each charge.
Lawyer T. Daneish, representing Murugan, sought a lower bail, saying his client was a former e-hailing driver, the sole breadwinner of his family and suffered from a heart condition.
The court set bail at RM46,000 and fixed Sept 9 and Oct 13 for mention at the Sessions Court and magistrate's courts respectively.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Teenagers plead guilty to possessing stolen motorcycles
Article:
SUNGAI PETANI: Two 15-year-old boys pleaded guilty in the magistrate's court here today to separate charges of possessing stolen motorcycles.
The pleas were entered before magistrate Noor Farah Hazwani Osman after the charges were read out separately.
They were charged under Section 411 of the Penal Code with possessing two stolen Honda C100 motorcycles belonging to two individuals at the Surau Taman Mutiara compound here at 11pm on July 14 and 15.
The offence carries a jail term of between six months and five years, as well as a fine, upon conviction.
One of the teenagers also pleaded guilty to a separate charge under Section 414 of the Penal Code for dishonestly disposing of a stolen Honda C100 motorcycle at the Surau Taman Mutiara parking area at about 11pm on July 15.
The offence carries a jail term of between six months and seven years, as well as a fine, upon conviction.
Prosecuting officer Insp Farah Amira Mohd Hassim conducted the prosecution, while both accused were unrepresented.
The court allowed bail of RM200 for the teenager facing one charge and RM400 for the other facing two charges, with one surety each, and fixed Sept 8 to receive social reports on both teenagers before sentencing.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 473 prison prospects undergo TVET training in construction since 2024
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A total of 473 prison prospects underwent Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) in the construction sector from 2024 to June this year, equipping them with skills to facilitate their reintegration into society upon release.
Deputy Home Minister Datuk Seri Dr Shamsul Anuar Nasarah said of the total, 184 prospects participated in the programme in 2024, followed by 234 in 2025, with another 55 prospects participating up to June this year.
He said the TVET training programme in prisons was a strategic initiative by the Home Ministry to enhance the employability of prospects and strengthen their rehabilitation ahead of their release.
"The training programme is supported by three main certification bodies, namely the Malaysian Skills Certificate (SKM) from the Department of Skills Development under the Human Resources Ministry, the Construction Skills Competency Certificate from the Construction Industry Development Board (CIDB), and the Modular Certificate through industry collaboration.
"For the construction sector, training under the CIDB certification programme covers courses such as welding, electrical work, mobile crane operation, plastering and other construction-related skills," he said during a question-and-answer session in the Dewan Negara today.
He said this in reply to a question from Senator Datuk Salehuddin Saidin on the government's plans to provide TVET or skills training programmes in the construction sector for prison prospects approaching the end of their prison terms, particularly through strategic collaboration with private companies and government-linked companies (GLCs).
Shamsul Anuar said 33 institutions under the Prisons Department had been recognised as Industrial Vocational Centres (PVIs), with more than 430 GLCs and private companies, including SWCorp, Lotus, Shopee and Micro Driving Academy, collaborating with the department.
Responding to Salehuddin's supplementary question on the effectiveness of the programme, he said records showed that almost 100 per cent of prospects who completed TVET training secured employment upon their release.
"Records showed that many prospects were trained by companies and continued working with the same companies after their release. This achievement reflects the Prisons Department's continuous commitment to strengthening the programme," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Civil servants must remain politically neutral during elections - PSD
Article:
SEREMBAN: Civil servants have been reminded to remain politically neutral and refrain from siding with any party, particularly during election periods, to preserve the integrity and professionalism of the public service.
Public Service Director-General Tan Sri Wan Ahmad Dahlan Abdul Aziz said civil servants must continue to uphold the core principles of the public service and remain loyal to the government of the day while carrying out their duties impartially.
"My advice to civil servants is that we must always remain neutral because we uphold the principle of loyalty to the government of the day.
"That is the principle we adhere to, and we cannot take sides with anyone during elections," he told reporters after the Madani Civil Service Aspiration Dialogue (Negri Sembilan Series 2) today.
Wan Ahmad Dahlan said the Public Service Department (PSD) has not, to date, received any reports of civil servants campaigning for political parties or taking leave to participate in campaign activities during the 16th Negri Sembilan state election.
He said the primary responsibility of civil servants is to serve the public professionally, regardless of political differences.
"Our duty is to provide the best possible service to the people. As long as it benefits the public, that is the only message I wish to convey to civil servants," he said.
The Election Commission has set Aug 1 as polling day for the Negri Sembilan state election, while early voting is on July 28.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: TH properties earn sustainability accolades for enhanced asset management
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Lembaga Tabung Haji (TH), Malaysia's Islamic pilgrims' fund, has received two accolades for its flagship properties, Menara TH Tun Razak and Menara Bank Islam, reflecting its continued focus on sustainable asset management, operational efficiency and long-term value creation.
Menara TH Tun Razak won the Non-Strata Office category, while Menara Bank Islam was recognised in the Single-Owned Office in a Mixed Strata Development category at The Edge Malaysia Best Managed & Sustainable Property Awards (BMSPA) 2026.
The awards recognise TH's efforts to manage and enhance its property portfolio through professional management practices, technology adoption and sustainability initiatives aligned with environmental, social and governance (ESG) principles.
Both properties are managed by TH Properties Sdn Bhd, a wholly owned subsidiary of TH.
TH chairman Tan Sri Abdul Rashid Hussain said the recognition reflected the organisation's commitment to managing its assets responsibly while ensuring they continued to generate sustainable value for its more than 9.8 million depositors.
"These awards go beyond recognising excellence in building management. They reflect TH's commitment to meeting the highest standards in all that we do.
"As an institution entrusted with safeguarding the savings of the Muslim community, every asset must be managed responsibly to deliver long-term benefits to our depositors, the community and the nation," he said.
He added that the recognition also demonstrated TH's capability as a multiple award-winning institution to integrate mature asset management practices with modern technology, digitalisation and sustainability initiatives that meet leading industry standards.
Menara TH Tun Razak, a 38-storey office tower on Jalan Tun Razak designed by architectural firm Hijjas Kasturi, has been a Kuala Lumpur landmark since its completion in 1985.
As part of TH's five-year asset management plan, the building has undergone a series of upgrades to improve operational efficiency, sustainability and long-term asset value.
The improvements include upgraded building facilities, a cashless parking system with automatic number plate recognition, enhanced safety and accessibility features, as well as plans to install a solar energy system to reduce energy consumption.
Meanwhile, Menara Bank Islam, a 34-storey office tower completed in 2010 and officially opened in October 2011, was developed by TH on charitable waqf land as an income-generating waqf asset and serves as Bank Islam Malaysia's headquarters.
The building's facilities management has been strengthened through proactive maintenance, condition-based monitoring and the implementation of the i-MANS computerised maintenance management system, improving operational efficiency, reducing response times and enhancing the management of critical building systems.
TH said these initiatives have reinforced the long-term reliability, resilience and sustainability of its property portfolio.
The approach has also contributed to a 6.6 per cent reduction in energy consumption against the adjusted energy baseline through measures such as building automation, optimising air-conditioning operations and adopting energy-efficient lighting solutions.
Rashid said the latest recognition reinforced TH's commitment to continuously improving its asset management framework while embedding ESG considerations across its operations.
"TH will continue to leverage technology and innovation to ensure our strategic assets remain competitive while delivering sustainable long-term value to our depositors," he said.
He also expressed his appreciation to all TH Group employees for their dedication and commitment to ensuring the organisation's assets are managed to the highest standards, earning industry-wide recognition.
TH posts highest payout in eight years
TH said it is in a significantly stronger financial position than it was in 2018 following years of recovery and restructuring, which have strengthened its balance sheet and enhanced its resilience.
The improved financial position has enabled TH to continue fulfilling its mandate of managing the savings of the Muslim community while consistently delivering world-class haj services.
TH said its stronger balance sheet has enabled it to maintain the cost of performing the haj unchanged for three consecutive years, from 2024 to 2026, while also announcing its highest profit distribution in eight years for the 2025 financial year.
The pilgrimage fund increased its profit distribution rate to 3.5 per cent for FY2025, up from 1.25 per cent in FY2018, benefiting more than 9.0 million depositors.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATE] 'Not a new stance': Malaysia reaffirms on non-recognition of Israel to US
Article:
REMBAU: REMBAU: Malaysia has officially reaffirmed its longstanding policy of not recognising Israel to the United States Department of State.
Foreign Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan said Malaysia's ambassador to the United States, Tan Sri Muhammad Shahrul Ikram Yaakob, had been contacted by the US Department of State to seek clarification on the matter.
He said the ambassador explained that Malaysia's position was neither new nor a policy shift, but one that was consistent with the country's long-standing immigration and foreign policies.
"Malaysia has never recognised Israel or the Zionist regime. Therefore, the implementation of our immigration policy is consistent with that position," he told reporters after attending the Santai Bersama Tok Mat Hasan programme in Pedas.
He said Wisma Putra had also engaged with the US Chargé d'Affaires at the US Embassy in Kuala Lumpur.
"Both sides have agreed to manage this matter in the best possible way," he said.
Mohamad said the Department of State had acknowledged and respected Malaysia's long-standing policy of not recognising Israel.
"As such, the issue of Malaysia purging Jews, as claimed, does not arise. If a person holds an Israeli passport, Malaysia does not recognise it, in line with national policy. It is not because they are Jews," he said.
Recently, eight US congressmen urged Washington to suspend military training funds for Malaysia unless Putrajaya reverses within 15 days what they described as a "discriminatory policy" following Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim's remarks that Israeli citizens in the country would be tracked down and deported.
In a letter to US Secretary of State Marco Rubio on Tuesday, the congressmen called for the suspension of the International Military Education and Training (IMET) programme, which provides professional military training for Malaysian military personnel in the United States.
On July 15, Anwar said Malaysian authorities would track down and deport any Israeli nationals found to be involved with the Network School following reports of alleged Israeli links to the project.
Subsequently, the Johor government revoked the Network School's operating licence yesterday, forcing it to cease operations.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MoE to increase funding for Asean Islamic science camp
Article:
NILAI: The Education Ministry is prepared to allocate more funding for next year's Asean Islamic Science Camp (AISC) to expand the programme and allow greater student participation, Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek said.
She said the initiative reflects the ministry's commitment to nurturing young talent through international programmes that promote excellence in science, technology and innovation.
"We regard these children as precious gems and valuable national assets.
"The additional allocation will help increase student participation and strengthen planning so AISC can become one of the country's key educational programmes," she said when officiating the closing ceremony of AISC 2026 here today.
Fadhlina said strengthening Science, Technology, Engineering and Mathematics (STEM) education and Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) remains a national priority.
She also called for greater efforts to foster a culture of STEM and innovation among students through a holistic approach that balances scientific learning with character development, humanitarian values and personal growth.
The seven-day AISC 2026, which began on July 17, brought together 165 students aged 13 to 16 from 22 schools and institutions.
Held under the theme "Empowering Young Muslim Scientists for a Sustainable Future", the camp served as an experiential learning platform integrating naqli knowledge (the Quran and Hadith) with aqli knowledge (rational thought) in science, technology and innovation.
The programme also focused on developing scientific skills, halal science applications, sustainability, research and innovation among gifted and talented students.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Cops bust solar cable theft syndicate, 9 held
Article:
KUANTAN: Police have busted the "Geng Lakino Garuda" syndicate with the arrest of nine men suspected of involvement in 21 cases of solar cable and telecommunications component theft, involving losses exceeding RM900,000, following a series of raids in Pahang and Selangor.
Acting Pahang police chief Datuk Azry Akmar Ayob said those arrested comprised four Malaysians, four Indonesians and one Chinese national, aged between 26 and 52, during operations conducted from June 27 to July 17.
He said the raids were carried out by the D4 Division of the Criminal Investigation Department (CID) at the Pahang police headquarters in Kuantan, Shah Alam and Sepang, Selangor.
"Of the nine suspects arrested, six are believed to have been directly involved in the theft of solar cables and telecommunications components, while the remaining three were buyers of the stolen items," he told a press conference at the Pahang police headquarters here today.
He said investigations revealed that the 21 theft cases reported between February and June this year involved five cases in Pahang, eight in Perak, five in Negri Sembilan and three in Terengganu.
Azry Akmar said police are still hunting two other suspects believed to be at large.
Investigations found that two of the suspects had previous criminal and drug-related records, while urine tests showed that seven had tested positive for amphetamine (AMP) and methamphetamine (meth).
He said police also seized items worth about RM500,000 during the operation, including a bonded lorry, a Toyota Hilux, more than 1,000 electronic chips of various sizes, as well as cables and components belonging to Tenaga Nasional Bhd (TNB).
Also seized were three machetes, three Touch 'n Go cards and nine mobile phones of various brands.
He said the case is being investigated under Section 379 of the Penal Code for theft and Section 411 of the Penal Code for dishonestly receiving stolen property.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: No fiscal hoards: MOF redirects RM2 bil diesel subsidy savings into direct public aid
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Savings generated from the targeted diesel subsidy mechanism are not being retained solely as a fiscal surplus but are instead being channelled into strengthening targeted assistance programmes, the Finance Ministry (MOF) said.
The ministry said the savings have been used to enhance programmes such as Rahmah Cash Aid (STR), Rahmah Basic Aid (Sara) and other initiatives, while supporting ongoing price monitoring and enforcement efforts to ensure subsidies and assistance reach their intended recipients and minimise leakages.
"The government will continue to monitor the implementation of the Budi Madani Diesel (Budi Diesel) programme and carry out improvements from time to time to ensure assistance is distributed fairly, effectively and in a targeted manner to those who truly need it," the ministry said in a written reply to the Dewan Negara published on the Parliament website.
The reply was in response to Senator Mohd Hasbie Muda, who asked about the government's follow-up measures after lowering the subsidised diesel price to RM2.10 per litre to ensure reduced operating costs translate into lower prices for goods and services.
The ministry said the targeted diesel subsidy implemented under the revamped Budi Diesel programme has delivered two key outcomes: ensuring a stable domestic diesel supply while allowing eligible Malaysians to continue purchasing diesel at a subsidised price.
"The revamp is expected to generate subsidy savings of up to RM2 billion annually, enabling the government to channel part of the savings towards reducing the subsidised diesel price from RM2.15 to RM2.10 per litre," it said.
The ministry was also responding to Senator Datuk Nelson W. Angang, who sought details on measures to ensure the targeted diesel subsidy does not burden the logistics sector, rural communities in Sabah and Sarawak, or the national economy amid global geopolitical pressures.
On June 22, the government announced the nationwide standardisation of diesel prices and subsidy mechanisms, effective July 1, 2026, allowing eligible Malaysians to purchase subsidised diesel at RM2.10 per litre through the Budi Diesel programme.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ex-bank analyst jailed three years for illegal investment activities
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Kuala Lumpur Sessions Court has sentenced former bank analyst Mohd Arif Fadzlee Mohd Arifin to three years in prison for unlicensed regulated activities and the unlawful use of the unit trust consultant title.
Arif was originally charged by the Securities Commission (SC) on Jan 23, 2024 with two charges for offences under section 59(1) and 362(3) of the Capital Markets and Services Act 2007 (CMSA).
For the first charge under section 59(1), Arif was charged for holding himself out as a representative of CIMB Wealth Advisors Bhd in relation to the regulated activity of dealing in securities, despite not holding a Capital Markets Services Representative's Licence (CMSRL) from the SC nor being a registered person.
"Dealing in securities is a regulated activity as set out in Schedule 2 of the CMSA and any person carrying out this activity is required to be licensed by the SC," said the SC
The second charge came under section 362(3) for misusing the unit trust consultant title which created the belief that he was licensed to deal in securities which he was not.
The offences took place in Petaling Jaya and Nilai in 2011.
Arif claimed trial to both charges and was released on bail of RM60,000.
At the trial, the prosecution called 10 witnesses, including two victims.
On Feb 27, Sessions Court judge Hamidah Mohamed Deril ruled that the prosecution had proven a prima facie case and ordered Arif to enter his defence on both charges.
In delivering its decision today, the court found that Arif failed to raise a reasonable doubt in the prosecution's case.
He was subsequently convicted and sentenced to three years imprisonment for each charge, with both jail terms to run concurrently.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Why New York's Mamdani cannot have Israel's Netanyahu arrested
Article:
NEW York Mayor Zohran Mamdani said this week that he had determined he lacked the legal authority to have Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu arrested for alleged war crimes should he visit the largest US city, after earlier saying he was considering the move.
Mamdani did not elaborate on how he had reached that determination.
Independent legal experts told Reuters that Mamdani lacked authority to have Netanyahu arrested because the US generally recognises immunity for heads of state and United Nations representatives, and because Washington is not a member of the International Criminal Court.
Here is a look at the legal issues:
WHY DOES MAMDANI WANT NETANYAHU ARRESTED?
The International Criminal Court, based in The Hague, issued an arrest warrant for Netanyahu in 2024 for alleged war crimes during the Gaza war.
Israel rejects the court's jurisdiction and denies committing war crimes.
In a New York Times interview broadcast on Saturday, Mamdani called Netanyahu a "war criminal" and said the city's legal department was actively considering whether the law would allow his arrest should Netanyahu visit New York for the United Nations General Assembly in September.
Mamdani, a Democrat, had made similar remarks while campaigning for mayor ahead of the Nov 4, 2025, election.
In a video posted to social media on Tuesday night, Mamdani said he had concluded he had no such authority.
"It is clear that we do not have the independent legal authority to enforce this warrant," Mamdani said.
Neither Mamdani's office nor Israel's embassy in Washington immediately responded to requests for comment.
WHAT DOES THE LAW SAY?
Experts in international law said there were multiple reasons that Mamdani could not order Netanyahu's arrest.
Since the United States is not a member of the ICC, there is no mechanism under US law to enforce the Hague-based court's arrest warrants, said Alexander Whiting, a professor at Harvard Law School and a former ICC prosecutor and Justice Department official.
Additionally, a 2002 US law called the American Service-Members Protection Act specifically prohibits the US from extraditing anyone, regardless of their nationality, to the ICC, said Whiting.
Sitting heads of government like Netanyahu are also generally immune from arrest and prosecution in US courts, and representatives of UN member states have legal immunity while transiting to and from a UN meeting, said Rebecca Ingber, a professor at Cardozo Law School and former US State Department lawyer.
CAN THE FEDERAL GOVERNMENT ARREST NETANYAHU?
In his video, Mamdani called on the federal government to execute the warrant.
In order for the federal government to arrest Netanyahu, the US would first need to join the ICC and repeal the American Service-Members Protection Act, Whiting said.
Even then, the agreement between the UN and the US conferring immunity on UN representatives would still be an obstacle, Whiting said.
US President Donald Trump, a Republican, said in a social media post on Monday that Netanyahu would not be arrested while in the United States.
* The writer is from Reuters
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MBSB sells all MIDF Amanah shares to Tortoise Works
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: MBSB Bhd, through its unit Malaysian Industrial Development Finance Bhd (MIDF), has divested all its stake in MIDF Amanah Asset Management Bhd (MAAM) to Tortoise Works Sdn Bhd.
The bank said the divestment is in line with its transformation strategy to sharpen its core business and is not expected to have any material financial impact.
Group chief executive officer Rafe Haneef said MAAM has established a strong reputation over the past 34 years and expressed confidence in the team's future as it enters its next phase of growth.
Tortoise Works director Shan Kamahl Mohammad said the company is committed to preserving MAAM's legacy while pursuing sustainable growth, strong governance and strong investment outcomes.
The Securities Commission approved the divestment on July 9, and the transaction was completed today.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Govt steps up mental health intervention for civil servants
Article:
SEREMBAN: The government is intensifying early intervention efforts to address mental health issues among civil servants after the MyPsych study identified more than 40,000 public officers as being at risk of negative behaviours, including suicide attempts.
Public Service Director-General Tan Sri Wan Ahmad Dahlan Abdul Aziz said heads of departments have been instructed to work closely with psychologists to provide early intervention for officers identified as being at risk.
"Intervention measures are being implemented immediately across all departments and ministries. We are concerned that if these issues are not addressed promptly, they could worsen and lead to serious incidents.
"In my view, this matter must be managed effectively so that our officers remain mentally healthy and can continue delivering quality services to the public," he told reporters after the Negri Sembilan Madani Public Service Aspiration Dialogue (Series 2, 2026) here today.
He said the government is also examining organisational factors, including workplace environment and organisational culture, that may contribute to stress among civil servants, rather than focusing solely on individuals experiencing mental health challenges.
To support these efforts, Wan Ahmad Dahlan said the "Happy at Work" programme has been introduced to encourage department heads to foster a more positive, supportive, and conducive workplace environment, thereby reducing the risk of mental health issues.
Although the latest survey showed a slight increase in the number of officers identified as being at risk, he expressed confidence that prompt intervention, including individual counselling sessions with psychologists, could help address the problem effectively.
Earlier in his speech, Wan Ahmad Dahlan called on civil servants to enhance productivity, commitment, and a high-performance work culture to ensure that government policies are implemented swiftly and effectively, in line with the aspirations of Malaysia Madani.
He said the various welfare initiatives introduced by the government should be reciprocated through improved performance and stronger commitment to the Public Service Reform Agenda.
He said the public now expects government services to be faster, simpler, more transparent and customer-oriented, making public service reform a necessity rather than a choice.
"To meet these expectations, the government has entrusted the Public Service Department (PSD) with leading efforts to implement the Government Service Efficiency Commitment Act 2025," he said.
He also commended the Negri Sembilan state administration for introducing various initiatives to safeguard the welfare of civil servants, including interest-free loan schemes for the purchase of personal computers and vehicles.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Rubio says International Criminal Court has 'lunatics, crazies'
Article:
MANILA: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio said on Thursday there were "lunatics, crazies" in the International Criminal Court and there was no way any US political or military officials would ever go on trial as the country was not a signatory to its treaty.
"If people want to sign and be a part of that stupid organisation, they can. But we're not going to be a part of it, and they're not going to apply their jurisdiction to Americans," he told reporters in Manila.
On Wednesday, member states of the International Criminal Court will vote on Friday on whether to fire chief prosecutor Karim Khan for alleged sexual misconduct in a case that has damaged the tribunal's credibility at a time when the US has ramped up its attacks on the court.
Khan, 56, denies any wrongdoing. The vote on his fate comes just a week after Washington announced a renewed diplomatic campaign to dismantle the ICC, which it says is a threat to US sovereignty.
"The court is already in a precarious state," Mark Ellis, the executive director of the International Bar Association and a longtime ICC observer, told Reuters.
In another development, two Ghanaian nationals have petitioned the ICC to open a preliminary investigation into attacks against foreign nationals in South Africa, which they said could constitute crimes against humanity.
South Africa has been gripped by a surge in anti-immigrant protests in recent months, accompanied in some cases by violence against foreigners, whom protesters blame for problems such as crime and unemployment.
The petition, which has been seen by Reuters and was submitted to the court on July 15, alleges a "pattern of widespread and systematic attacks" against migrants in South Africa and accuses President Cyril Ramaphosa's government of failing to prevent or punish those crimes, including murder.
It was filed by Palgrave Boakye-Danquah, a former Ghanaian government spokesperson on governance and security, and Emmanuel Kotin, a counter-terrorism and security analyst.
"For years, African brothers and sisters have been killed, beaten, and driven from their homes in South Africa," they said in a press release.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar committed to resolving Kampung Sungai Cot Orang Asli land issue
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim will continue to facilitate efforts to resolve the land dispute involving the Orang Asli community in Kampung Sungai Cot, Maran, Pahang.
Political secretary to the Finance Minister Muhammad Kamil Abdul Munim said Anwar would raise the matter with the Pahang government to help secure a swift, fair and lawful resolution.
He said Anwar had taken note of the issue and personally contacted community representatives to hear their concerns.
"The Prime Minister said that the matter would be brought to the attention of the Pahang government, as land administration and ownership fall under the state government's jurisdiction under the Federal Constitution.
"The Prime Minister has no authority to direct the state government on land matters. Nevertheless, he has never remained silent," he said in a statement today.
Kamil said attempts by certain parties to politicise the issue should not distort facts or constitutional principles, as this could hinder efforts to find a solution.
He urged the Pahang government to act swiftly, adding that the rights and welfare of the Orang Asli community should not be affected by further delays.
"They deserve a fair and dignified resolution without further delay," he said.
On Friday, Anwar said in a Facebook post that he would
discuss
the matter with the Pahang government as it falls under state jurisdiction.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ex-bank analyst gets jail for posing as unit trust consultant
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A former bank analyst was sentenced to three years' jail today after the Sessions Court found him guilty of dealing in securities without a licence and unlawfully using the title "Unit Trust Consultant" (UTC).
Judge Hamidah Mohamed Deril handed down the sentence after finding that Mohd Arif Fadzlee Mohd Arifin had failed to raise reasonable doubt against the prosecution's case.
Arif was convicted under Section 59(1) of the Capital Markets and Services Act (CMSA) for posing as a representative of CIMB Wealth Advisors Bhd and dealing in securities without a licence.
The offence carries a maximum fine of RM5 million, imprisonment of up to five years, or both.
He was also convicted under Section 362(3) of the CMSA for using the title "Unit Trust Consultant", which implied he was licensed to deal in securities when he was not.
The offence carries a maximum fine of RM1 million, a jail term of up to five years, or both.
The offences were committed in Petaling Jaya and Nilai, Negri Sembilan, in 2011.
During the trial, the prosecution called 10 witnesses, including two victims.
Deputy public prosecutor Annarina Chacko Jacob, together with prosecuting officers Mohd Shafiq Azman, Eunice Ong and Sharifah Noor Effah Syed Ahmad Fuad, prosecuted the case, while Mohamad Salihen Mastor represented Arif.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Court jails former bank analyst over fake investment adviser claims
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 23 — The Sessions Court here today sentenced a former bank analyst to three years’ jail after finding him guilty of unlicensed regulated activities and the unlawful use of the Unit Trust Consultant (UTC) title.
The Securities Commission of Malaysia (SC) in a statement said that Judge Hamidah Mohamed Deril meted out the sentence against Mohd Arif Fadzlee Mohd Arifin after finding that the accused had failed to raise a reasonable doubt in the prosecution’s case.
The court sentenced him to three years’ imprisonment for each of two charges, with the jail terms to run concurrently.
According to SC, Mohd Arif Fadzlee was originally charged on Jan 23, 2024, with two charges for offences under Section 59(1) and 362(3) of the Capital Markets and Services Act 2007 (CMSA).
“Mohd Arif Fadzlee claimed trial to both charges and was released on bail of RM60,000. On Feb 27, 2026, the court ruled that the prosecution had successfully proven a prima facie case and ordered him to enter his defence on both charges.
“At the trial, the prosecution called 10 witnesses, including two victims. However, Mohd Arif Fadzlee elected to testify under oath and called no other witnesses in support of his defence,” the commission said.
For the first charge, Mohd Arif Fadzlee was alleged to have held himself out as a representative of CIMB Wealth Advisors Berhad in relation to the regulated activity of dealing in securities, despite not holding a Capital Markets Services Representative’s Licence (CMSRL) from the SC nor being a registered person.
He was charged under Section 59(1) of the CMSA, which carries a fine not exceeding RM5 million or a maximum imprisonment of five years, or both.
The accused was also charged for misusing the UTC title, which created the belief that he was licensed to deal in securities which he was not, under Section 362(3) of the same Act, which provides a fine not exceeding RM1 million or imprisonment not exceeding five years, or both.
Both offences took place in Petaling Jaya and Nilai in 2011.
The SC was represented by Deputy Public Prosecutor Annarina Chacko Jacob and Prosecuting Officers Mohd Shafiq Azman, Eunice Ong and Sharifah Noor Effah Syed Ahmad Fuad, while Mohd Arif was represented by counsel Mohamad Salihen Mastor. — Bernama

Title: Highway to the animal kingdom: WCE adopts Taiping Zoo's resident gaur
Article:
TAIPING, July 23 — West Coast Expressway Sdn Bhd (WCE) has contributed RM10,000 to the Taiping Municipal Council (MPT) in support of wildlife conservation efforts through the Zoo Taiping & Night Safari (ZTNS) Animal Adoption Scheme.
WCE chief executive officer Lyndon Alfred Felix said the contribution, made under the company’s corporate social responsibility (CSR) programme, would help cover the care costs of a 12-year-old male ‘seladang’ or Malayan gaur named Bima, which weighs more than 200 kilogrammes.
“We chose the gaur as WCE’s mascot because it symbolises resilience, strength and endurance, values that reflect the aspirations and work culture we seek to instil within our organisation.
“In addition to sponsoring the gaur’s care, the contribution also covers two other initiatives – a mural depicting the WCE alignment and the development of the WCE Pocket Park to enhance visitors’ comfort and experience at Zoo Taiping,” Lyndon told reporters after the ‘WE CARE’ CSR programme at the zoo here today.
MPT president Mohamed Akmal Dahalan was also present.
Lyndon said WCE’s commitment to environmental conservation is also reflected in the construction of two Animal Box Culverts (ABCs) along Section 11 of the WCE through the Gunung Bubu Forest Reserve in Kuala Kangsar to enable wildlife to move safely across the area.
The initiative reflects WCE’s approach to balancing infrastructure development with biodiversity conservation, in line with the company’s commitment to sustainable development and Environmental, Social and Governance (ESG) practices.
Meanwhile, Mohamed Akmal said WCE’s contribution demonstrated the important role the corporate sector could play in supporting wildlife conservation while strengthening ZTNS as one of the country’s leading tourist attractions.
He described the initiative as an example for more companies to support conservation efforts and help ensure the welfare of animals at the zoo.
“Contributions like this are invaluable because the cost of caring for animals, including providing food, is high. Amid the current economic challenges, support from the corporate sector helps ease some of the financial burden,” he said.
On the Animal Adoption Scheme, Mohamed Akmal said it had received support from several corporate organisations to help fund the care of animals at ZTNS.
Current sponsors include Comfort Rubber Gloves Industries Sdn Bhd, iCare RX Sdn Bhd, Yayasan Kossan and the Malaysian Palm Oil Green Conservation Foundation (MPOGCF).
“Comfort Rubber Gloves Industries sponsors a Malayan tiger, iCare RX a tapir and Yayasan Kossan a sun bear, with each contributing RM10,000.
“This year, in addition to WCE’s sponsorship of the gaur, MPOGCF has also contributed RM10,000 towards the care of a Bornean orangutan,” he added. — Bernama

Title: CZBUCG held multiple meetings with Penang govt before tunnel project tender, MACC witness says in court
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 23 — The Sessions Court today heard that four to five meetings were held between Beijing Urban Construction Group (CZBUCG) and the Penang government regarding the state’s major roads and undersea tunnel project before the open tender process began.
Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) Senior Superintendent Zulhilmi Ramli, 34, testified that the meetings were attended by CZBUCG representative Datuk Lee Chee Hoe and a representative from the Penang State Economic Planning Unit (UPEN), identified as Azlan.
The 38th prosecution witness made the statement while being cross-examined by defence counsel Mohd Haijan Omar during the corruption trial of former Penang Chief Minister Lim Guan Eng over the RM6.3 billion major roads and undersea tunnel project.
However, Zulhilmi said he could not recall the exact dates of the meetings.
Mohd Haijan put it to the witness that there was no basis for claims by the CZBUCG witness regarding any meetings beyond the three already identified, but Zulhilmi disagreed.
When asked what evidence supported the existence of additional meetings apart from those held on May 10, May 19, and July 11, 2011, prior to the open tender process, Zulhilmi said his conclusion was based on the testimony of the 23rd prosecution witness, Datuk Zarul Ahmad Mohd Zulkifli, who had stated that preliminary meetings had taken place.
However, Zulhilmi agreed with the defence that he did not know what had been discussed during those meetings and that there were no official records documenting meetings between CZBUCG and the Penang government concerning the project.
During earlier proceedings on June 5, Zulhilmi dismissed Mohd Haijan’s suggestion that the three meetings between CZBUCG and the Penang government on May 10, May 19, and July 11, 2011, had not been specifically arranged by Lim.
The witness also agreed that no documents were seized directly from Zarul Ahmad during the investigation.
When asked what documents had been obtained, Zulhilmi clarified that the documents were seized from Zarul Ahmad’s company through an individual identified as Mr Azli, rather than directly from Zarul Ahmad himself.
He further agreed that the seized materials mainly comprised payment vouchers, cheque books, and files related to the project.
Lim, 65, is charged with using his position as Penang Chief Minister to obtain a gratification of RM3.3 million by allegedly helping a company owned by former Consortium Zenith Construction Sdn Bhd director Datuk Zarul Ahmad Mohd Zulkifli secure the RM6.341 billion Penang major roads and undersea tunnel project between January 2011 and August 2017.
He also faces a second charge of soliciting 10 per cent of the profits expected to be earned by Zarul Ahmad as an inducement for helping the company secure the project.
The offence was allegedly committed near The Gardens Hotel, Lingkaran Syed Putra, Mid Valley City, between 12.30 a.m. and 2.00 a.m. in March 2011.
In addition, Lim faces two charges of causing the disposal of two plots of Penang government land, valued at RM208.8 million, to developer companies linked to the undersea tunnel project.
